item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: Noting that the image used in this article was taken by the author, and therefore is cc-compliant with the SCP wiki’s license (CC BY-SA 3.0).

item.title: A good story
item.content:encoded: I feel like this does everything right. It presents the mystery of what's so weird about these that they need to be SCPs, and as we find that out, we're also given this bittersweet story about a time traveler who found a place for himself even stuck in the distant past. Would +2 if I could, but you'll have to settle for a +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Y'know, I totally missed that. Explains the comment below, too. I just figured it was some weird alternate dimension, and now I like this even more.

You can't put pieces together if you're incapable of reading them. c.c;

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Realm of dogs and parrots? That's… not what I got from the article at all. My interpretation is that they guy went back in time and uplifted a bunch of dinosaurs. Said dinosaur's just happen to kinda sound like dogs/parrots…. talking dogs/parrots.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ayyy this got posted! Happy to see it doing well. Nice job!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I really like this. Not too long, nor too obtuse. I like the story it tells. Poor guy never gets home, dies over 70 million years in the past. But he's a rock star!

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think mostly I'd just rather find out that this guy has been taken to a realm of dogs and parrots from context in the logs. I like piecing things like that together myself. :B

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I dunno. I kinda like the implication of 'If you're reading this, you're part of the Foundation - and thus likely know what a talking dog sounds like'.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I knew I had a second nitpick which I'd forgotten about! I agree with TL333s. That line came across as odd.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
SCP-3834-2 has a distinctly inhuman voice, which most closely resembles that of a canine or psittacine.

1) What does a canine voice sound like? Specifically speaking intelligible English.

2) Don't bother answering that, because I think you ought to take out that bit of description and just let the dialogue in the excerpts speak for itself. Bury that lede a little deeper.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Author, your article image is 4.3 MB. For a 300 px wide image, that's crazy unnecessary. Do you mind if I shrink that image down so it's not murder on mobile devices with limited data plans?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I also got Hitchikers Guide vibes from this- the introducer reminded me of the host at the restaurant at the end of the universe “but seriously folks..”

Minor nitpick- I find non-humanoid containment chambers a strange description. I don’t think it’s clear what that means. I would suggest changing it to standard containment locker (I might be having a brain fart, but I believe that that’s a term commonly used on the site for containment for smallish inanimate objects).

Anyway I found this enjoyable +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Reminds me of the humorous absurdity of "The Hitchhiker's Guide to the Galaxy". Quite entertaining and it still works as a "normal" SCP.
